title: Enable dataset restoration after rebuild and provide personal-use code documentation

requirements.text: Persist the uploaded spare-parts dataset and maintenance password across backend canister upgrades so that rebuilding/redeploying the same canister does not erase previously uploaded Excel data (subject to the existing 7-day retention logic).

quotes: Rebuild now and get the uploaded previous excel data

acceptanceCriteria: After a backend upgrade/redeploy (that preserves canister state), calling getAllSpareParts returns the same records as before the upgrade, unless the retention window has expired.
After a backend upgrade/redeploy, getRetentionStatus returns a consistent timeRemaining relative to the original uploadTimestamp (not reset by the upgrade).
After a backend upgrade/redeploy, verifyMaintenancePassword still validates against the current password (not reset to the default).

requirements.text: Add a client-side backup/restore flow so a user can recover previously imported spare-parts data even if the draft app expires and the backend no longer has the dataset: (1) automatically save the last successfully imported dataset (the parsed SparePart array) into browser storage, (2) expose UI actions to download a local backup file (JSON) and to restore from the saved backup by re-uploading it to the backend after unlocking with the maintenance password.

quotes: Rebuild now and get the uploaded previous excel data

acceptanceCriteria: After a successful import, the app stores a local backup of the imported spare parts in browser storage.
The Upload area provides a clearly labeled action to download the locally stored dataset as a JSON file.

If the backend returns zero parts but a local backup exists, the Upload area provides a clearly labeled action to restore/re-import the local backup to the backend (requires the existing unlock/password flow).
Restoring from backup updates the table and import status to match the restored dataset.

requirements.text: Provide personal-use (self-managed) code and run instructions in-repo, including how to run locally, deploy to the Internet Computer with dfx, and how data persistence/backup works (including limitations of draft expiry vs live deployments). All user-facing documentation must be in English.

quotes: I need this software coding for my self

acceptanceCriteria: Repository contains clear English documentation explaining how to run the frontend locally and deploy the backend canister using dfx.
Documentation explains how spare-parts data is stored, how long it is retained, what happens on upgrade, and how to use the new backup/restore flow.
Documentation includes a brief section explaining Draft vs Live environments and the impact on data availability (at a practical, user-action level).

constraints: Backend must remain a single Motoko actor (all logic in backend/main.mo).
Only create backend/migration.mo if required by the existing-state upgrade policy; otherwise use Motoko upgrade hooks/stable variables within backend/main.mo.

Do not edit any paths listed as immutable: frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.tsx, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, frontend/src/components/ui.
nonGoals: Do not add third-party authentication providers beyond Internet Identity.
Do not add external databases or non-IC storage services for backups.
Do not implement real-time sync or multi-user collaboration.
